title: Eremophila deserti
intro: Eremophila deserti, also known as the "Desert Emu Bush," is a shrub native to Australia, characterized by its small, tubular flowers and woody stems. This plant has been traditionally used in Australian folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Eremophila deserti include iridoid glycosides, phenylethanoid glycosides, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its medicinal activities. Eremophila deserti is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are believed to aid in the treatment of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
taxonomy: Eremophila deserti is a species of flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, however more recent classifications place it within the family Scrophulariaceae but in some classifications it is placed in the family Lamiales. It is a flowering plant native to Western Australia. This species is part of the Eremophila genus which contains over 200 species. The Eremophila genus is closely related to the genus Myoporum. The classification of Eremophila deserti can vary depending on the source.
